string 2: Look...!

So, the pro-X Program faction has run into the
anti-X Program faction... This can't be good.

Selection is a natural law. The X Program is a test 
bestowed upon us from on high...

...so that we might progress towards the next stage
in our evolutionary development as a species.

The X Antibody is no instrument of divine will.
It is a program, no more and no less.

You seriously believe yourselves to be chosen beings?
Your arrogance knows no bounds.
I can see no value in further conversation.
Aw, they're fighting again?!

Everyone, please... This place is not our world. Can we not
at least work together until we are able to return home?

You should already know the answer to that.
Cooperation is out of the question.

We're going to do what we believe is right. 
And it seems they've reached a similar conclusion.

Yes, if we can defeat Parallelmon and make use
of their power, we can solve everything.
So... it comes down to a race.
Whoever defeats Parallelmon first will have their way.
What exactly are you saying?

And, in any case, Parallelmon will be no easy foe.
Unless we all work together—
Cowards need to keep their mouth shut.

There are two doors here.
We need not share the same path.

I couldn't agree more.
From this point on, it'll be a race.

Ugh... Why can't we work together,
even at a time like this?
Hee hee...
What's so funny?

They're just so hopelessly naive!
Immature! Idealistic!

Each group believes in their own sense of justice—
that theirs is the one true way.

How many conflicts have broken out from a disagreement
on what's right? So, even here, history repeats itself.

The world of Digimon is not so different from our own. 
Truly fascinating...!

Well, as far as we're concerned, we need to
get them to band together. Otherwise...

Yes... Otherwise, we probably won't be able to beat
Parallelmon, meaning we'll be stuck here forever.
We have to go after them.

The only question is, which way do we proceed? Through the
digital-looking door, after the pro-X Program group?
Or through the wooden door, after the anti-X Program group?

I know this place... It's Akihabara.
So... we're in our world?

It looks like more Akashic debris has appeared before you...
A different space must be fragmenting this time.

"Now, THIS is a lovely part...! And that one, just
gorgeous! Oh, ew! No. Bad part! No, thank you."

"I need to make that device a reality, or Digimon—
phase electron lifeforms—will prove a threat to humanity!"
Digimon? A threat?
Do Digimon attack humans in your world?

The sad truth is, a certain number of Digimon simply cause
trouble. If it were only the chosen, this would not happen.

Overpopulation is at the root of all of this. Though it pains
me to say it, the process of selection must continue.
Hee hee...
Their conversation didn't sound very funny to me...

Oh, no. Not funny at all. They were being completely
logical. Everything they said fits together perfectly.

Maybe a little too perfectly. They seem drunk on their own logic.
Perhaps that's why they consider themselves "chosen."

If you ask me, I'd say that conversation of theirs
was overly logical. There was no joy in it.
And... that's a problem?
Of course!

Without some spark of hope, something to make the heart
leap, their claim of an evolutionary step forward is a sham.

We need to tell them that. Which is just another
reason we should keep going after them.

Something about this place seems... familiar.
Would you not agree, Omnimon?
There is a certain aspect of this world that resembles our own.

The tension in the air is reminiscent of that following
the conflict that broke out regarding the X Program.

I'm curious. Could you describe for me, in concrete terms,
how exactly the X Program works?

It lodges itself within a Digimon,
then ultimately deletes it from existence.

The only way to escape its effects
is to possess the X Antibody, which is—
Y-You, there! Hand over your X Antibodies!

Since possessing the X Antibody is a matter of life
and death, there are many who try to take it by force.
These Digimon were likely sent here by Parallelmon as well.
Beware! They are on the attack!

W-We're sorry! 
It was wrong of us to attack you!

We won't try to steal them anymore,
so please, just let us keep our antibodies!
It seems their X Antibodies are extrinsic rather than intrinsic.
Extrinsic? So there are different types of X Antibodies?

Yes. Aside from a few special cases, most Digimon
acquire the antibody extrinsically.
Which is where selection comes in.

It may be sad, but we must accept that this trial bestowed
upon us will lead to the evolution of Digimon as a species.

The X Antibody completely nullifies the X Program,
but it does not last forever.

After it has been used up, the X Program will
continue to act as a force for natural selection.

Survivors must battle amongst themselves
for the X Antibody in order to stay alive.

I see. So those Digimon just now were risking their lives
in an attempt to acquire the antibody.
How brutal... It's like a scene straight out of hell.
No, this is a trial bestowed upon us that we must overcome.

The weak shall die... But if that is the divine law we must
live by, I wish to at least make it so they do not suffer.
Yes, that is the compassionate thing to do.
They can't be serious...!

Their thinking was never so extreme
before they acquired the X Antibody...
Could the antibody itself be affecting their personalities?

There are plenty of examples of bodily changes having an
impact on personality, though they're all in human cases.

Hm... I am sensing the presence of others. Most likely, there are
more Digimon who are after the X Antibody lurking nearby.

Justice must be served, so what we do will not change. 
If you try to stop us, we may end up in quite the rivalry.​​
What is this place? This... is not our world.

No, but I know this lab like the back of my hand... Looks
like our world is next.
"You want me to leave public safety with you?"

"Yes... I have some concerns about the organization,
which I suspect you share."

"I've already written my resignation. Ever since Captain
Kuremi quit, I feel like things have gotten out of hand."

"When he was still around, it felt like we had purpose...
But I'm surprised to hear that you're leaving, too, Dr. Simmons."
"I thought... Don't you see Digimon as enemies?"

"I did, until very recently. I believed preparing for the
coming threat of the Digimon was the right thing to do."

"But depending on the situation, the right thing to do
can change. Clinging to a single vision of justice is foolish."

"...Which is exactly what the public safety office
is doing right now."
"That's right. I see you're every bit as sharp as I suspected."

"That test subject Captain Kuremi's friend brought in... 
Aegiomon, I think it was?"

"That Digimon clearly had his own will, his own emotions. 
More importantly, he trusted the humans he was with."

"Seeing that caused me to reevaluate my understanding.
Or, rather, you could say that those two taught me something."

"They showed me that Digimon and humans
can understand one another."
"What's so funny?"

"The wonders never cease. What you said just now didn't 
sound like data or facts. It sounded like a dream of yours."

"What is science for but to make people's hopes and dreams 
come true? I'm sure that's what Dr. Yuki would say."
"I... don't want to create instruments of war."

Instruments of war...?
Did Digimon start a conflict in your world?

They played a crucial role in the outbreak of one.
That much is objective fact.

But please don't misunderstand.
I don't blame Digimon at all.
I believe we can find a way to coexist—

There is no perfect future where all can be happy
and live in harmony.

Such lies will save no one.
It's just as I have been saying: selection is a necessity.

There are too many unnecessary Digimon in this world...
Your future is the result of that.

We need the X Program, and with Parallelmon's ability,
we can carry out the process of selection more efficiently.
More efficiently...? What do you mean by that?

We can isolate all lesser beings in another dimension.
That will resolve everything.

But that is absurd! How can you even consider
such a monstrous "solution"...?!

Apologies, but we aren't interested in a debate.
If you wish to try and stop us by force, we won't hold back.

Doesn't seem like there's any common ground
to be found, at all.
Maybe we won't be able to cooperate after all...

That's what happens when folks get too fixated
on one idea of what's right.

They talk of evolution, but the true meaning of that term
involves adapting to fit an environment.

For those who insist they don't need to change,
there can be no evolution.
"So, they haven't found Kuroi, either..."

"That's right, sir... I'm afraid we've lost
many of our old comrades."

"Some were caught up in the explosion over the government building.
Others died because of anomalous phenomena..."

"There are also lots of people who are still unaccounted for,
but there's one thing we can say for certain."

"Every one of them was doing all they could
to accomplish their mission."

"So I can't just sit around doing nothing.
Dr. Simmons and I are returning to Anomalous Affairs."

"The public safety office is barely even functional anymore,
but somebody has to do something..."
"Mr. Kuremi... No, Captain Kuremi..."
"...what are you going to do?"

"I'm going to fight. I'm going to face down our foes,
however terrifying the enemy Digimon may be!"
You would fight Digimon?
I... won't deny what I've done.

I've taken up arms against Digimon many times now,
so that we humans might survive.
Hate me if you will. You have every right.
Honestly, Asuna! Did you have to put that so bluntly?
Though I suppose that IS rather in character of you...
Still... I haven't given up.
Given up? On what?
Coexistence.

You would speak of such a thing? You, who have
taken up arms against Digimon? How absurd!

In truth... you are every bit as self-centered
as those other cowards!
"...Parallelmon?"

"That's right, sir. They're a hostile Digimon we haven't
been able to get much of a handle on so far."

"Dr. Simmons has a theory that they can
travel freely through space-time."

"I see... Then we'll need to take action
as soon as possible."

"Man, just the thought of traveling through space-time gets my
head spinning. Makes me wonder what's even real anymore."

"Do you remember what you said to me
back when I was still new to public safety?"
"I signed up because I wanted to do what was right."

"But once I got within the bounds of the organization,
I started to question what the right thing to do really was."

"I wasn't even sure if it was humanity
that we should be protecting anymore..."

"I was lost, I was worried, and I couldn't see my way through.
But you noticed that, and decided to encourage me."

"You told me that I was exactly the right
kind of person to work in public safety."

"That none are more trustworthy than those who question what is 'right.'
That nothing is more terrifying than an arbitrary sense of justice."

"That some things matter more than using righteousness
as a weapon... such as always questioning what is just."

"And that as such, to have doubts about
these things is its own kind of skill."

"If it weren't for those very words, I wouldn't be standing
here today, continuing to question what is right."

Some things matter more than using
righteousness as a weapon... Hm...
What is it?
Sorry. It's nothing. Never mind.

Countless fragments of space-time lie buried
beyond an Akashic Backdoor.

Apparently, some of that debris was taken
from times and places related to you.

While not very strong, there's a force that draws beings back
to the parts of space-time that they were cut off from.

As a side effect of that, you're all basically acting like
magnets, drawing specific bits of debris toward you.

I suppose you could say that's the mechanism at work
behind the phenomena you're seeing now.
"The research data on Parallelmon has been stolen?!"

"Yes... and it was Kuga who took it! That data could be the 
key to a fundamental theory of travel through space-time."

"I don't know his motives, but this is a disaster!
That data could've been our ticket out of this hellish scenario!"
That memory is from just before we came here.

The two of us went after Kuga, and just when we
finally found him, Parallelmon showed up.
Kuga was probably sent here as well.

Research data on Parallelmon...
Travel through space-time...
That will prove useful information indeed!
